prose:
Method–provenance block
Property: Atom-condensed Fukui functions (f⁺ for nucleophilic attack, f⁻ for electrophilic attack, f⁰, and dual descriptor) and identification of the most electrophilic/nucleophilic sites of formaldehyde.
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` ≡ B3LYP/def2-TZVP, RI (density‐fitted) in PySCF.
Geometry provenance: user-supplied geometry taken as-is from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz; no further optimisation (single-point calculations at N, N+1 and N–1 electron counts ⇒ DFT//input-XYZ protocol).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvation).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (charge 0, multiplicity 1) for the N-electron state; cation run at charge +1, doublet; anion run at charge –1, doublet.
Software: chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill (backend: PySCF 2.x, invoked via `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py …`).
Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles for all three charge states converged; integrity.status = "ok", integrity.trustworthy = true.  Warnings returned by the engine are reproduced verbatim below.

Key results (Mulliken-charge scheme, atom order O(1)–C(2)–H(3)–H(4))
• f⁺  = [0.46813824, 0.70839130, –0.088269392, –0.088260145]
• f⁻  = [0.46742464, 0.11244422,  0.21007563,  0.21005551]
• f⁰  = [0.46778144, 0.41041776,  0.060903121, 0.060897683]
• Dual descriptor Δf = [ 0.00071359555, 0.59594709, –0.29834503, –0.29831566]

Most electrophilic (largest f⁺): atom 2 (C) with f⁺ = 0.70839130.
Most nucleophilic (largest f⁻): atom 1 (O) with f⁻ = 0.46742464.

Live log & files: run streamed to a timestamped `fukui_<timestamp>.out` in the current working directory while executing; result JSON saved to `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp5n_muaco/live.json`.
